summary: Bearer Token Authentication Migration E2E Testing - All tests PASSED. The migration from HTTP-only cookies to Authorization: Bearer token with localStorage is working correctly in both backend and frontend.
critical_code_review_comments: Backend security.py correctly implements cookie-first, then Bearer token fallback for backward compatibility
Frontend utils.js apiRequest() correctly reads session_token from localStorage and sets Authorization: Bearer header
Frontend AuthContext.js correctly saves session_token to localStorage via setAuthUser()
Login flow correctly redirects to Emergent Auth in preview environment (no GOOGLE_CLIENT_ID in frontend/.env)

context_for_next_testing_agent: Bearer token auth migration is complete and tested. Key endpoints tested: /api/auth/me, /api/auth/logout, /api/commesse/, /api/invoices/, /api/auth/download-token. All work with Authorization: Bearer header. Cookie fallback also works for backward compatibility.
